stories.title: Tehran launches more strikes after explosions reported in southern Iran
stories.body: Centcom says it hit 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of strikes, with the Iranian health ministry saying 14 people were killed since Tuesday.

stories.title: Big fall in oil, gas and cargo ships taking US-backed Hormuz route after new strikes
stories.body: Data shows a decline in the number of ships - many carrying oil and gas - going through the waterway after attacks this week.

stories.title: Democrat Graham Platner suspends campaign for key US Senate race after assault allegation
stories.body: The decision came days after a woman accused him of sexual assault, an accusation he says is "categorically false".

stories.title: Ukrainian agent accused of murdering Monaco bomb suspect changes story
stories.body: Two men with links to Ukraine's security services are accused of killing Anastasiia Berezovska and dumping her body in woods.

stories.title: Palestinians mourn Gaza World Cup screenings organiser killed in Israeli strike
stories.body: Aid worker Mohammed al-Wahidi had become a prominent humanitarian figure during the Israel-Hamas war.

stories.title: Greek F-16 fighter jet crash-lands at busy Zakynthos airport
stories.body: The runway is closed after the jet reportedly landed without deploying its landing gear before bursting into flames.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes Russian ships near Crimea, escalating attacks on fuel supplies
stories.body: The attacks appear to be the latest phase of Ukraine's bid to choke off supplies and routes into and out of occupied Crimea.

stories.title: Ex-Olympian pleads not guilty to Reflecting Pool vandalism charges
stories.body: A former US Olympian canoeist denies accusations that he deliberately damaged the lining of the refurbished Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.

stories.title: Starmer gifted gun and ammunition by Turkish president at Nato
stories.body: The weapon has not been brought back to the UK and has instead been left with British officials in Turkey.

stories.title: Wealthy AI workers send San Francisco house prices soaring
stories.body: The median cost of a home in the city is now $1.7m, a record high, according to the latest figures.

stories.title: Arizona toddler found alive in morgue after being declared dead from drowning
stories.body: After he was discovered to still be alive, the 18-month-old was flown to another hospital and survived.

stories.title: Nato allies announce £37bn for new missile project
stories.body: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer will convene around a dozen leaders to discuss the programme in Ankara.

stories.title: Justin Bieber joins Madonna, Shakira and BTS for Fifa World Cup final half-time show
stories.body: The Super Bowl-style show marks the Canadian singer's latest big performance after his comeback shows at Coachella.

stories.title: Factory fire kills at least 28 in China's 'shoe capital'
stories.body: Footage shows huge flames rising from a building and thick, black smoke rising into the sky.

stories.title: International court tells BBC of breakthrough in Sudan war crimes probe
stories.body: The ICC has been looking into atrocities committed in Darfur over the past three years.

stories.title: Teenager arrested after two girls, 13, seriously injured in German school attack
stories.body: Police said a major operation was under way in the area of Welfen-Gymnasium secondary school in Schongau, Bavaria.

stories.title: Ukraine to get licence to produce Patriot missiles, Trump says
stories.body: Although the Patriots are highly effective against Russia's ballistic missiles, they also have lengthy production times.

stories.title: It was 'love at first sight' with their adopted baby. Then they were told he may have been trafficked
stories.body: When they adopted Marcus, David and Ally's dream of having a child was complete - but now they could lose him.

stories.title: A country that made huge progress on measles now reports 120,000 cases
stories.body: Hundreds of children are dying from measles in Bangladesh, where hospitals are overwhelmed.

stories.title: Platner's disastrous candidacy exposes rifts that could dampen Democrats' Senate hopes
stories.body: Along with creating tumult in a must-win race for Democrats, Graham Platner's exit is laying bare division between the party's left wing and moderates.

narrative:
🟡 **Portfolio Mood: Cautious** — Today's signals reflect a mix of incremental progress and underlying instability, with a focus on survival and stability-oriented developments. While some breakthroughs are evident, contested dynamics and unresolved tensions persist.

---

**1. Structural Progress Amid Persistent Volatility**  
The International Criminal Court's reported breakthrough in the Sudan war crimes probe is a notable positive signal, reflecting incremental progress in accountability for conflict-related atrocities. This development, while encouraging, remains embedded in a broader context of instability in Sudan, where systemic violence continues to challenge governance and humanitarian conditions. Similarly, Ukraine's anticipated license to produce Patriot missiles signals a deepening of Western military support, reinforcing Kyiv's defensive capabilities. However, this escalation also underscores the entrenched nature of the conflict, with no immediate resolution in sight.

**2. Contested Signals Highlight Diverging Trajectories**  
The NATO allies' announcement of a £37 billion missile project reflects a significant investment in collective defense, aligning with achievement and knowledge-oriented priorities. However, the contested score suggests underlying debates about the strategic allocation of resources amid competing global crises. Meanwhile, the Greek F-16 crash at Zakynthos airport, while operationally minor, underscores the risks inherent in high-tempo military activities, particularly in regions with overlapping civilian and military infrastructure. These signals collectively point to a world where defense priorities are intensifying but remain fraught with operational and political complexities.

**3. Longer-Term Implications of Escalating Tensions**  
The supporting signals from the Gulf region, including Tehran's strikes and the reduced traffic through the Hormuz route, highlight the fragility of global energy supply chains. These developments, while not directly tied to today's top signals, amplify the broader theme of geopolitical contestation. Similarly, Ukraine's strikes on Russian ships near Crimea suggest a continued escalation in the Black Sea theater, with potential ripple effects on global trade and regional stability. These actions reinforce a trajectory of protracted conflict, where tactical gains are unlikely to yield strategic breakthroughs in the near term.

**4. What to Watch Next**  
Key areas to monitor include the operationalization of Ukraine's Patriot missile production and its impact on the balance of power in the region. Additionally, the ICC's progress in Sudan warrants close attention, particularly in terms of whether it catalyzes broader accountability mechanisms or remains isolated progress. Finally, the Gulf region's energy dynamics and the potential for further disruptions in the Hormuz route will remain critical, given their implications for global markets and energy security.

---

Today's signals reflect a world in flux, where progress in one domain is often offset by volatility in another. The cautious mood underscores the need for vigilance as structural tensions persist across multiple theaters.
